title: The Rot Infection
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|There are a lot of SPaG issues, my sir! Also has tonal issues (formating will be fixed soon!)|date=06/18/2026}}
[[File:1130-2.gif|2000px]]
{{Notice|body=CSS is coded to fit dark mode, so for the best experience with light mode, switch to dark mode to achieve optimal reading experience. Thank you, and enjoy the story. -Nocliped}} {{CSS|content= .page { border: #px solid/double/other—weight } }} {{CSS|User: Help I Nocliped/ Rot Infection.css}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: The Rot Infection}} {{Warning|border=Red|color=black|text=red|content=Some images may be triggering to some audiences. Furthermore, this page’s horror relies heavily on biology & body horror. If this warning concerns you, please skip this page.}} Oh, you're here. Thirsty for knowledge that you don't need. That you should not know. You humans are so weird. Why do you wish to know things that should not be known? Information that will keep you up at night, cause you to lose sleep, and eventually lead you to see me. You will run into me or one of my minions, no doubt. That’s the Backrooms for you, a cruel place where death shows its hand everywhere. But if you see me, And I see you, There will be no escape, no information that saves you, No help that will help you, no muscles that will evade me, Prey is prey, and I will not let go of that. No matter how far you run, I will be there. If you go to a different level, I will be there. If you go back to the Frontrooms, an apocalypse will start, and it will all be because you thought you could escape. So sit down and await my arrival. I'm going to catch up eventually, so why bother wasting your stamina? Why spend your last seconds in pain? You know, I'm really a nice guy if you think about it. If you cooperate, maybe I will make it painless. I have needs, so you can't beg for your life, because I'm famished. I mean, have you ever fasted for a year? I could eat a human every day, but I choose not to. I only kill if I need to. And you can thank my friend for that, the only logical human I know. In my first stages, the power consumed me. I felt so much of it, and I went on a rampage, killing everyone in sight. I loved the feeling of being powerful. I loved the feeling of having my stomach full after it had hungered so much in my journey through the Backrooms. Despite all of my power, I was no better than a common hound, blinded by hunger, blinded by power. As I feasted on humans, a lack of them started to appear. They became few and far between; the first signs of an apocalypse were showing. And I was too blind to realize my power was killing everyone off, Then one day, like usual, I spotted a human. I was going to attack and kill him, but he set up a trap that I fell for. At that moment, I knew that my life was in his hands. I was prepared to die. But he was not like me, no. Instead of killing me, he talked, and I heard him. I considered his words, and as I took a moment, I realized that I finally got a bit of my old self again. It was beautiful, not because I was good, I'm still far from that, this is not a fairy tale, It's because I learned that a muscle is no good if there is no thought controlling it, Now I feast a few and far between because the more I wait, the easier it is to find the next meal. I gained more power, And I feel better than ever. I don't even hunger now, that's what my new power gifted me, though I still have to eat. So you can run all you want, But we will come face-to-face soon, no doubt about it, Just remember, not all stories have a happy ending. —RoT (This note was found in Level 4 with a trail of black ink trailing away from it. It's safe to assume that this was probably the RoT. If one has more information about the note, we urge you to come and talk to the M.E.G. Thank you.) {{Fullscreen|show=>Data found, open file?|hide= X|content=
ENTITY 61 — The Rot Infection
Frequency: 6/10
Intelligence: 4/10
Threat:6/10
Aggression: 8/10
The rot is an entity that should not be messed with; flee if found.
== Description == The Rot Infection is a virus-based entity that targets the nervous system and takes over the body. This virus is considered one of the great hazards of the Backrooms, with most encounters being lethal. The entity has two forms, the virus and the rotted, with subforms branching out from them. The two forms are the Infection Virus form and the Rot Victim (or Rotted). The first form, or the virus form, is a basic group of 1–5 viruses whose only job is to find a host. Like most viruses, once inside the body, they start infecting cells to obtain nutrients and multiply. These viruses still do this, but in a different manner, which will be further expanded on later. The second form is the Rot Victim, also known as the Rotted form, which occurs when the virus fully infects a victim. In this form, the virus is still inside the body, controlling it, but because of the drastic changes it causes to the person, we classify it as a different form. This is also because this form includes other subforms that may appear if the circumstances are met. Once the infection takes hold, you can identify an infected victim by looking at their skin, mouth, and eyes. Their skin will develop dark spots, their mouth will move closer to the left side of the face and grow larger, and their eyes will shrink and be pushed toward the right side of the face. There are also 3 key stages of the infection process, as shown in the image. {{Blurimage Border|file=ROT ROT ROT ROT ROT.jpg |caption=An image demonstrating the process until a victim is in Rotted form.|width=150px}} The way the virus and its victims function is quite odd and poorly understood, and so far only basic information and observations have sufficed. These things eat by collecting something called rot, which is anything that deforms the body, such as cuts and broken bones. Rot can be collected through physical contact between the virus and/or the Rotted form and a host already infected with rot. Once the collection process starts, these wounds will begin to heal. After the entity is done, poison will be injected into the wanderer, leaving them with only 5 minutes to live. It's also important to note that a Rotted can cut a wanderer and collect that rot, which is why their mouths have many teeth. How this thing can take rot from people and convert it into a food source is a gray area, to say the least, though it is believed to be similar to plants getting food from the sun. However, this is not confirmed. What we do know is that this rot collection process is extremely painful for the person undergoing it. We also know that the whole process takes about 30 seconds per source of rot, so if someone has 5 cuts, then it will take about 2 minutes and 30 seconds. For the sake of clarity, we will now focus only on the rot infection victims, or Rotted, unless specified otherwise. == Behavior == Rot Infection victims seem human from a distance because the host’s characteristics are still visible, though their gait is choppy and resembles that of someone who has drunk too much. Rot Infection victims are easily identified up close because of the random dark spots on their skin and their large, displaced mouths. It is also important to note that they still look somewhat human, but trust us, the person you see is not the person you knew, so kill on sight. Additionally, the Rot victims cannot see, but they can feel vibrations in their ears and soft tissue as a replacement for sight. They also have all four of their other senses. . They behave like a drunk person with a delayed reaction time. There are a few reasons for that. The first is that because they cannot see and rely on sound and vibrations, knowing where they are going is hard. The most blatant reason is that the virus infecting the brain has to learn how to control the person with little to no senses, helping it. But once they lock onto a target, trying to sneak away is better than attacking them, because even though you may land hits, you will have to kill the brain to kill the entity. Furthermore, the entity predicts where you will be to make up for the lost reaction time, so getting hit back with a harder and deadlier force is common. Don't let their movement fool you; they are still powerful. Also, they are very aggressive and willing to kill anything, especially because they cannot really see what they are killing. They tend to target smaller creatures like kats, kid wanderers, and sometimes Hounds, though even if you are big, they will not think twice about trying to kill you. == infection process & survival tactics== Below is an accurate amount of time it takes for an infected person to reach their full rot form:
Stage Time Notes Symtoms
Virus Planting 0min-1min At this time, the virus has been planted in the victim, but the infection has not fully begun yet. N/A
Infection Start 1min-20min The virus starts to attack your immune cells and collect the wounds it sustained, converting them into rot to feed on. N/A
sytoms start 20min-30min At this time, the victim's white blood cells have sustained enough damage to cause pain. A random acidic pain.
Virus Multiplies ~1 hour This is the only point where your immune cells can kill off the virus because the virus will infect a red blood cell and use it to multiply. If your white blood cells kill the infected blood cell, you will be ok A alarming feeling of tranquility
Brain take over begins ~1.5 hour After the virus multiplies to about 50 new viruses, it will start to attack a part of your brain that controls thoughts. Body starts to get a high fever
Brain take over ends ~2 hours At this point,t the victim will lose all thoughts and is now considered dead victems eyes go glassy and black spots start to appear
rapid multiplication ~3 hours now that the person can't kill themselves, 50% of the viruses go down the bloodstream to start to kill and multiply in blood cells More random black spots appear & jaw hangs open
killing of the last of the cells ~5 hours At this time, there are over 600,000 new rot cells, and the killing of the extra cells will start The majority of the victims will start to turn a void black
Body transformation commences ~7 hours over 25% of the new cells will transform into building cells, and %10 percent of the low cells will turn into ripper cells to decompose the skin and muscle random tryphobic holes will start to appear near the jaw
other cell get jobs ~9 hours now that the construction of the new body commences new cells will start to arise with 25% of the cells turning into a attacking cells 5% of them turning into repairing cells 10% of them turning into viral cells and 30% of the rest stay idle ready to transform if a shortage of cells arises jaw gets expanded and pushed to the left of the face while the eyes shrink and get moved to the right
End of cycle ~24 hours This is the final stage where the face has been transformed fully, with 50% of the face being a mouth while the eyes are pushed almost to the end of the face. Now the viruses are not transforming and are ready to hunt End of transformation
There are some simple survival tactics to prevent the virus and ways to kill/avoid the Rotted. === Ways to survive the virus === There are some ways to reduce your risk of becoming fully infected with the virus. The first and easiest way is to avoid contact with rotted areas or random black spots on the ground and walls. This is because the Rotted spread the virus through their mouths, and if killed or injured, their blood is black due to the high levels of virus particles inside them. So, the best way to avoid the virus is never to let it enter your body. Furthermore, if you get the virus, there are really only two ways to save yourself. The first is to hope that your immune system takes care of the infection during stage ~1, but the odds of that happening are low, given that the virus has ways to hide. The other confirmed way to save yourself is by pouring 1–3 drops of Liquid Pain onto the infected area. This will only work if you have been in contact with the virus within the last 3 minutes; otherwise, you will have to drink 5 drops of Liquid Pain to attempt to expel it, though it is not a guaranteed cure. === Ways to survive/kill a Rotted === While the Rotted are harder to kill since you must destroy the source of power, aka the brain, to kill the entity fully, attacking their body will not work because they do not need human organs to regulate blood flow. The true way to kill one is to sever its head and destroy the brain. Guns also work if you fire 3–4 shots directly into the forehead. You cannot kill this entity with blunt force unless you somehow manage to rip off the head and remove the brain afterward. Furthermore, if you do not have any of the necessary weapons to kill one, follow B.A.O.S protocol (Back Away On Sight). As long as you remain silent, you can usually avoid confrontation. If someone does not have enough time to run away or is not being actively hunted, the best option is to hide in something like a crate and remain completely silent. Because they rely heavily on hearing, try to stay hidden for 10–15 minutes so that you are safely out of hearing range. ==Different cells== Each rot infection victim houses different cells, each with different jobs, and they all have different ways to help out around the victim's body. There are 5 different cells the virus can transform into if it collects enough rot. |-| Idle cells= {{Blurimage Border|file=Idle Cell.jpeg|caption=A image of the idle virus cell.|width=150px}} Idle cells are cells that were just made or put aside so that they can be transformed in times of need. Idle cells look like blobs with a mouth in the center to collect food. They are a dark black color. These cells make up around 10%–20% of all cells in the body. These cells have one of two jobs: one, they have no purpose because they just came from a normal human cell. Or two: they are put aside in times of need, so if more attacker cells are needed, some of the idle cells can become attacker cells. They are also used to transform the Rotted into different forms. The cells know when to transform if any one of them approaches and gives them a specific protein, so they know they need to change and into what. Furthermore, they are the only cells that cannot infect a person, so coming into direct contact with them will not harm one. Despite their looks, they are harmless and cannot even attack. They linger only in the bloodstream, waiting for a signal to transform. |-| Infection cells= {{Blurimage Border|file=Infection Cell.jpeg|caption=A image of the infection virus cell.|width=150px}} The infected cells are responsible for transmitting the Rot Infection. Infection cells are dark, black, bacteria-looking cells with little arms that they use to grab onto things. Other than that, they have a mouth at the top to collect rot. These cells make up 5% of all cells, no matter what, with 3% lingering in the bloodstream and the rest in the mouth. The infected cells' only job is to spread the virus, so if a cut opens up, they instantly find it and leave the body to try to find a host. If there is no cut, they will instead hang out in the mouth, so if the Rotted finds a meal, they can try to infect that person if they do not die or get away. Aside from trying to infect victims, they are passive and cannot kill anything other than human cells (red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets, etc.). Other than that, they are completely defenseless and can even be killed with Almond Water. Additionally, if less than 0.5% of infected cells remain in the body from loss of blood flow, the rotted body will shut down and die. While other cells can infect another host, they mostly die to the immune system, thus limiting their persistence. |-| Attacker cell= {{Blurimage Border|file=Attacker Cell.jpeg|caption=A image of the Attacker virus cell.|width=150px}} Attacker cells are responsible for killing off human cells and fighting off infections. The attacker cells look like infection cells with a lighter tone and no arms. Instead, they have spikes and many mouths to collect food and kill attackers. The cells make up 35% of all cells, and if that number gets over 50%, it will trigger the Rotted body to transform. The attacker cells' job is to terminate anything that might directly threaten the body. If they deem anything as a threat, even their own kind, they will send out chemical signals to get more attacker cells to kill the threat. Like humans, these cells have a small chance of becoming cancerous or infected, leading them to become delusional and attack their own cells. In some rare cases, they might even try to kill the brain, which in turn will kill the host, leading all cells to die. Despite these cells killing off most threats, they are very susceptible to death, having little to no defense against attacks, so even Almond Water can kill them. Furthermore, they do not eat, and they starve to death after about 1–3 weeks. Additionally, if the population of attacker cells dips below 5%, the body will slow down until the population rises again. During that time, ripper cells will become the new attacking cells, though they are not very effective. |-| Builder cells= {{Blurimage Border|file=Builder Cell.jpeg|caption=A image of the builder virus cell.|width=150px}} Builder cells are the most important cells. They look like gray circles with black tentacles jetting out from their bodies. They are also coated in a slimy coating so they can move faster. These cells make up 30% of the body, with 50% of them focused on repairing wounds and moving body parts from the host, and the other 50% controlling the body of the Rotted. While these cells are very diverse and can do many jobs, their main two jobs are repairing wounds and moving body parts, while the other is controlling the brain and directing chemical and protein signals. The first job is the reason the rotted wounds heal so fast: as soon as an open wound forms, builder cells head over to fix it. Unlike a normal human body, they do not scab over the wound, allowing infected cells to leave the body. Instead, they make new skin cells using proteins from the body. Once a wound is healed, they go back to the brain. These builder cells, along with fixing wounds, are also the ones who move and expand the mouth once the muscles have been loosened by the ripper cells. The other job is to control the brain and body, and the reason these cells do that, compared to other cells, is that they can produce and receive electrical signals, move fast, and slide through cracks. With those two skills combined, flocks of these cells gather around brain regions that control movement. As with all other cells, if the population dips below 85% of its usual level, critical failure will occur, and all cells and the Rotted will die within a week. |-| Ripper cells= {{Blurimage Border|file=Riper Cell.jpeg|caption=A image of the ripper cell.|width=150px}} Ripper cells are responsible for the destruction of hard tissue. These cells have a mix of teeth for grinding and tentacles for moving or tearing. Other than that, they are a void-black color and have tiny spikes all over them to improve grip. These cells make up 5% of the body; if they all disappeared, nothing would really happen. But if they make up more than 10% of the body, they will transform. The cells' jobs are pretty obvious, but they help rip and move tissues. They are the most important cells during the early development of the Rotted because if they cannot do their job correctly, the builder cells cannot move the muscle tissue, which is too tough for them. Eventually, the Rotted will die of hunger. They also help develop the teeth in the mouth by mixing certain proteins found in the body. The last job they have is to step up and protect the body if most of the attacker cells are eliminated, but this is very rare, and there have been only 2 confirmed cases. == Different Forms == |-| Tentical form=
Form #1– Tentacle form
Frequency: 2/10
Intelligence: 4/10
Threat:9/10
Aggression: 10/10
The tentacle form is a powerful form of the rotted
The tentacle form is very powerful and highly hazardous. The tentacle form appears to be a large humanoid creature with tentacles jutting from its mouth, replacing its feet and fingers. The creature is mostly black, with only very minimal patches of human skin showing. Because of how much energy it takes to form all of these tentacles and its new height, there will have to be a population of 73% of all virus cells being builder cells, and the rotted would have had to eat a large meal before the transformation to supply enough energy. Before the rotted can transform, it must find a safe space to rest and conserve energy. Once it finds its spot, it will become completely immobile and will not move, even if it is actively going to die. So if one sees a rotted standing completely still and twitching every so often, kill it immediately before it enters its new form. If the rotted manages to survive about 2–3 weeks of idleness and is not in its new form, it will begin to hunt larger, more dangerous creatures like skin stealers and humans and will tend to ignore smaller prey like kids and hounds. Furthermore, with its newfound tentacles, it can now hunt more easily because the inward-facing spikes on every surface of its tentacles keep victims trapped and shred skin to convert it into rot. There is little to nothing you can do to escape its tentacles. Additionally, if one wants to kill the rotted in this form, they will have to rely on guns, as close-range weapons will not be effective due to the tentacles. One to five rounds fired directly into the brain will be enough to end its life. |-| Ripper form=
Form #2— Ripper form
Frequency: 1.5/10
Intelligence: 6/10
Threat: 9/10
Aggression: 10/10
The ripper form is a powerful form that comes from the rotted.
The ripper form looks like a fully black, all-black human with many mouths and teeth all over its body. There is little to no human skin left on this creature. All of its teeth are gray, with a black goo covering the tips. The circumstances needed for this form to occur are that the ripper cell population exceeds 50% and a good meal is consumed. The necessary energy will then be sufficient to start the transformation. Just like the tentacle form, the rotted will start seeking a safe spot to idle so that the transformation can begin. Unlike the tentacle form, the transformation takes only 3 days, and during that time, the entity gets smaller rather than larger, favoring agility over strength. Because of its speed and close-combat-focused build, this form likes to run up to wanderers or sneak attack them around corners. It will then start to use the many teeth on its body to grind up their flesh. Because of their size, they tend to hunt smaller creatures, but they will also fight and kill larger animals, and humans are their main target. Surviving this form is extremely difficult, and the survival rate is low because of its speed (up to 30 miles per hour). Shooting them is close to impossible, so instead try to cut them with a blade and use that moment of shock to decapitate them. After that, remove the brain. |-| Mimic form=
Form #3— Mimic form
Frequency: 2.5/10
Intelligence: 3.5/10
Threat:7/10
Aggression: 10/10
The mimic form is a weaker form derived from the rotted.
The mimic is a very weak, rotted form that may present some hazards. The mimic form is just a decapitated rot head with black tentacles jutting from the neck and mouth. It often hides in crates. The circumstances that lead to this form arising are that someone has to sever the head of a rotted one and leave it alone. As long as there are >1000 builder cells, this now decapitated head will grow two short tentacles out of the neck stump. After that, it will start to move to find a box to hide in until it can close off all of the holes and grow more tentacles. Because of its weakness, it has to rely on ambushing its prey to kill it. They do this by closing a crate and waiting for someone to open it. Once that happens, the head will jump out and attack the unsuspecting victim. Due to this hunting strategy of waiting for someone to open the box, the only prey it usually gets is humans. Because of its immobility, the mimic form is very easy to kill. If one finds a crate, one can pour liquid pain into the cracks and wait a few minutes for it to die. You can also kill it by destroying the brain signals. |-| The RoT= Me? Why do you want to know about me? I already gave you some information, right? Is that not enough? Okay, so I see that you are stubborn, too blind to see that no amount of information will help you, but I will fill your need for information. You know, knowledge is dangerous if you think about it. The more you know, the more you want to find out. And then there is the other side, where you are informed too little, and your curiosity becomes the puppet master. No matter what, knowledge is as lethal as poison. It kills more than deadzone levels. But what about misinformation? You know, knowledge that is not true—that’s even worse than regular knowledge. If it is hidden well enough, everyone will prize it, love it, and follow it. If you are convincing enough, you can tell people to walk off a cliff, and they will follow. What do you think lawyers are for? If they are convincing enough, no matter how strong the evidence is, they can save a killer from death. What do you think politics does? They try to manipulate you into voting for them or siding with them. Heck, what do you think I am doing? I am trying to convince you of something with the most convincing tone I can. How can you even trust me? Who knows, the information might all be false. Who knows, maybe I am just a cruel prank to scare wanderers. Everything has strings behind it. Even if it's true, someone is writing something to make you do something. Even the supposed neutral form of writing—“informational”—silently convinces you to side with something by holding back and giving you information. Everyone, everything has a layer of information that hides its true self. No one knows the full story of anything. Only our god can know all of that. Even if you do not fully know yourself, there will always be something that you do not know. Humans evolved to bury guilt and lie to themselves to try to make their lives better in some way. That is the way information works. Even the true things are never fully true; there is always one piece of information that is held back. How can you truly trust anything? I intervened on this part of the page to withhold information I believe is false or not good. Please trust me that this information is sufficient. Just remember: information is like a thin fabric going through a field of knives. There will always be cuts and holes. No matter how many stitches you put in it, as long as you can cover up those holes, everyone gets impressed—even though there are still tiny holes yet to be addressed. And sometimes those holes get bigger or get discovered. That is information. So as you look through this, remember, there will always be holes… good luck. == RoT - The One RoT ==
[[File: Afjheao35873.jpg |140px]]
RoT— The One RoT
Frequency: 0.5/10
Intelligence: 9.5/10
Threat:10/10
Aggression: 3/10
Rot, also known as The Only Rot, is the first-ever rot victim and the most powerful
The Only Rot, or just RoT, was the first-ever rot victim. RoT appears as a 6’2 fully black humanoid figure, with a mouth of sharp teeth that occupies half his face and part of his chest. He has other mouths: two smaller 1’5-foot mouths on his chest and stomach, and a larger 2’3-foot mouth on his back. These are to allow him to collect rot from multiple people at once. Furthermore, RoT’s feet seem to melt into the ground, leaving black goo every time he moves. This goo consists of Infection cells looking for a new victim. This goo will evaporate and rejoin RoT given enough time. RoT's behavior is also very distinct, with a very light, poetic way of speaking; as you talk with him, you will also notice that he is very smart and knowledgeable, and may even seem playful to many people. However, because of this knowledge, he is very arrogant, which is expected given his power. Additionally, he seems to enjoy getting attention by accepting interviews often and bloating during them. It's important to note that he has the power to delete interviews if he deems them “boring,” as proven multiple times. Besides RoTs' looks and behavior, there is also his power to address, which is stronger than that of most common entities, such as skin stealers and humans. However, his power is limited to his knowledge, not his strength, so he is still just below the most powerful entities. Even thogh he cant beat most entities he can still evade them quite well being able to noclip at will and even morph his body in some ways because he is mostly liquid with no skin some of his other powers include: long rage rot collection with something called a helix tung, above average strength, a inhumane memory, knowledge of how to hack the most secure databases, ability to form limbs into weapons, and a very suppresed digestive system that allows him not to eat for years on end. {{Blurimage Border|file=Helix tung.jpg|caption=A image of the helix tung.|width=150px}} While there are other powers, like the ability to control rotted, they are not as important or as noteworthy as the other powers. It's important to know that he can still learn additional abilities, and this documentation will eventually become outdated as more studies on RoT come out. == Discovery & History == From an interview with RoT, or the first Rot victim, he said he was transformed into a Rotted around 1723 because he noclipped in 1720. While the information provided might not be accurate, there are documents and writings from that time describing an entity resembling the Rotted, so it is safe to assume that the first-ever Rot victim lived between 1723 and 1734. Other than that, the virus became more widespread in 1750, causing a massive, deadly outbreak. After 1750, cases began to decline as more people learned to protect themselves, though large outbreaks have occurred from time to time throughout history. The deadliest of those outbreaks occurred in 1943. This outbreak was called “The Black Rot Outbreak.” During this time, it is thought that over 2.5% of the entire Backroom population was either killed or infected. Because of this deadly outbreak, M.E.G. and other colonies started to band together to terminate the infection. This was called “The Great Clean Up.” During this cleanup from 1944 to 1948, over 20% of the entity population was eliminated. Still, despite many efforts, full eradication was deemed impossible, and the Great Clean Up operation was abandoned, being replaced by efforts focused on spreading information about how to survive. After that time, there was only one minor outbreak, which wiped out a colony of about 400 people in 2005. However, because of the eradication of the colony, the infected individuals eventually died due to starvation
== Additional notes == Congratulations, you just finished another page! Despite my warnings, you found out the knowledge you wanted, whether it is enough for you or too little, or you may believe it's a lie. Whatever you think, you have drunk the poison from the golden cup, and now you have to live with the consequences. As we wait and see if that poison will be digested in your stomach and pass harmlessly through your bowels, or seep through your stomach and burn everything inside you with curiosity, or worse… It is partly my fault, you know, adding that warning at the top. I made a mistake; that gave you too little information, which made you curious. Thus, you are still reading the page. It was rigged from the start. Since you are still here and I have nothing to keep from your eyes, I want to discuss why I am so opposed to your reading about us. . It's very simple. Do you know the saying “curiosity kills the cat”? There is a simple answer for that. If a cat gets too curious and steps outside its safe zone, nothing protects it from death. So if you read this and you want to know more, you are going to step out of the safe zone and get killed by either one of my victims or me, which is not something I want, because there is only so much fish in the pond, you know? You are exchanging your life for knowledge. Now do you see why it's so dangerous? I don't think I can put it more simply! You humans are so stupid. I mean, if you are so smart, why don't you hear my warnings after I explain clear, simple, true knowledge? If you don't listen, are you really that smart? If you walk into a trap and get stuck despite the warning signs, are you smart? If you can take the information and relax safely, that's smart, because you can't get cut if you don't pick up the knife. You can't fall if you never climb up. You can't choke on food if you never eat. That's the depressing truth, and if you can't open your eyes to realize the best defense is never going into battle— The best defense is never putting yourself at risk. The best defense is not a good offense, because that's how people die. And that's the truth. That no one wants to listen to.
|padding=2rem 15%|number=1|color=#000000}}

=Author:= [https://backrooms.fandom.com/wiki/User:Help_I_Nocliped Help I Nocliped] =Reader:= {{USERNAME}} =Art:= I made all art, help i nocliped =authors note= If you have not figured it out, the Rot process and the Rotted are very similar to how things rot in real life, so I think that’s a cool little Easter egg. And if you go even further, I made something that says they collect rot, like how plants collect sunshine. That’s intentional to show more about the food/rotting process.
